中文 EN
LLM Post-Training & Agentic AI Research 大模型后训练与 Agentic AI 研究
Zhihui Chen 陈致晖
Ph.D. Student in Artificial Intelligence, National University of Singapore
新加坡国立大学人工智能博士生

I research post-training for large language models — reward design, preference alignment (DPO/RLHF/RLVR), and verifier-in-the-loop evaluation — and build multimodal agent systems end to end: RL post-training pipelines, trajectory & preference data engines, and open-source agent harnesses with verification loops. Currently a Multimodal Agent RL research intern at ByteDance (Singapore); target role: LLM research intern. 我从事大模型后训练研究——奖励设计、偏好对齐（DPO/RLHF/RLVR）与验证器闭环评测——并端到端构建多模态 Agent 系统：强化学习后训练流程、轨迹与偏好数据引擎，以及带验证闭环的开源 Agent 框架。目前在字节跳动（新加坡）任多模态 Agent 强化学习研发实习生；目标岗位：大模型研发实习。

Post-Training & Alignment Research 后训练与对齐研究 Multimodal Agents · Clinical AI Applications 多模态 Agent · 临床 AI 应用
ByteDance (Singapore), now: Multimodal Agent RL — V-FIPO, first-author ICLR 2026 submission 字节跳动（新加坡）· 在职：多模态 Agent 强化学习 — V-FIPO，一作 ICLR 2026 在投
StepFun: StepAudio2.5 ASR pre-training data system — Speech-LLM Research Intern, 2025 阶跃星辰：StepAudio2.5 语音基座预训练数据体系 — 语音大模型研发实习，2025
MiniMax Cowork Team Fellowship — USD 4,500 compute grant for medical foundation-model agents MiniMax Cowork Team Fellowship — 4500 美金算力支持，医疗基座模型 Agent
View Publications 查看论文 View Showcase 查看项目展示 Google Scholar Download CV 下载简历
zhihui.chen(at)u.nus.edu GitHub LinkedIn

I am a PhD student in Artificial Intelligence at the National University of Singapore, supervised by Prof. Mengling Feng. My research centers on post-training for large (multimodal) language models — reward design, preference alignment (DPO/RLHF/RLVR), verifier-in-the-loop evaluation — and agent harnesses that hold up outside the lab. Healthcare is my primary application domain, not the boundary of my research.

Research: ACL 2026 & EMNLP 2025 main-conference papers, plus two first-author papers under review (ICLR & EMNLP 2026); open datasets and models on Hugging Face with 6K+ downloads.

Open source: Author of the smart-word-agent → Office Agent document-agent line: a ReAct tool-calling harness with a rendered-screenshot verification loop; 1K+ downloads in the first week.

Academic Service: 40+ completed reviews for ACL, NeurIPS, IJCAI, IEEE Affective Computing, ACM TIST; ACL 2026 Main Session Chair.

我目前是新加坡国立大学人工智能博士生，导师为 冯梦凌教授。 我的研究聚焦（多模态）大模型后训练：奖励设计、偏好对齐（DPO/RLHF/RLVR）、验证器闭环评测，以及能在真实场景落地的 Agent 工程。医疗是我的主要应用场景，而非研究边界。

研究：ACL 2026 与 EMNLP 2025 主会议论文，另有 2 篇一作论文在投（ICLR 与 EMNLP 2026）；在 Hugging Face 开源数据集与模型，累计 6K+ 下载。

开源：smart-word-agent → Office Agent 文档 Agent 系列作者：ReAct 工具调用框架 + 渲染截图验证闭环，发布首周 1K+ 下载。

学术服务：为 ACL、NeurIPS、IJCAI、IEEE Affective Computing、ACM TIST 完成 40+ 篇审稿；ACL 2026 主会议 Session Chair。

Featured Work 代表项目
View showcase 查看项目展示
Interpretable medical deepfake detection 可解释医学深度伪造检测

MedForge MedForge

A data-and-model framework that detects medically plausible image forgeries through localized evidence, expert-aligned reasoning, and a Localize-then-Analyze detector. 通过 MedForge-90K 基准、专家对齐推理和先定位再分析检测器，对医学影像伪造进行定位、判别和解释。

ACL 2026 Main ACL 2026 主会议 19 lesion types 19 类病灶 10 deepfake models 10 种伪造模型
Project Page 项目页面 Paper 论文
Medical foundation model development and clinically verifiable Agent workflows 医疗大模型开发与临床可验证 Agent 工作流

MiniMax Cowork Team Fellowship MiniMax Cowork Team Fellowship

A compute-supported project connecting long-context, multimodal, and Agent capabilities with medical foundation model development, data flywheels, and verifiable decision paths. 将 MiniMax 长上下文、多模态与 Agent 能力连接到医疗大模型开发、数据飞轮和可验证决策路径。

MiniMax Cowork Team MiniMax Cowork Team USD 4,500 compute grant 4500 美金算力支持 Medical Foundation Models 医疗大模型开发
Showcase 项目展示 MiniMax MiniMax
Document agent with a rendered-verification loop 带渲染验证闭环的文档 Agent

Office Agent Office Agent

From smart-word-agent (ReAct harness, 1K downloads in week one) to Office Agent: every edit is re-rendered by real Word/Excel and audited by an independent multimodal verifier, with plan-first execution, budgeted retries, snapshots/undo, and prompt-injection defense. 从 smart-word-agent（ReAct 框架，首周 1K 下载）到 Office Agent：每步编辑由真实 Word/Excel 重新渲染，交给独立多模态验证器审查，配合先计划后执行、预算化重试、快照/撤销与提示注入防御。

Render-Verify-Repair Loop 渲染-验证-修复闭环 Model-agnostic (Kimi-K2 / MiniMax-M3) 模型无关（Kimi-K2 / MiniMax-M3） 1K downloads / week 1 首周 1K 下载
GitHub GitHub Showcase 项目展示
Open Source · GitHub 开源项目 · GitHub
github.com/richardChenzhihui github.com/richardChenzhihui
smart-word-agent Agent Harness Agent 框架

ReAct document-editing agent — natural-language Word editing, 1K+ downloads in week one; evolving into Office Agent with a rendered-screenshot verification loop. ReAct 文档编辑 Agent——自然语言编辑 Word，首周 1K+ 下载；正演进为带渲染截图验证闭环的 Office Agent。

Python 9
ACL2026-MedForge ACL 2026 ACL 2026

Code and dataset of the ACL 2026 main-conference paper — interpretable medical deepfake detection with Localize-then-Analyze reasoning and Forgery-aware GSPO. ACL 2026 主会议论文的代码与数据集——先定位再分析推理 + Forgery-aware GSPO 的可解释医学深度伪造检测。

Python 10
med-banana-50k Dataset 数据集

Large-scale instruction-based medical image editing dataset with an editor–verifier–refiner agentic pipeline and LLM-as-Judge quality control. 大规模指令式医学图像编辑数据集，配套编辑-验证-精炼 Agent 流程与 LLM-as-Judge 质量控制。

Python 71
DivScore EMNLP 2025 EMNLP 2025

Zero-shot LLM-generated-text detection in specialized domains via normalized entropy scoring and domain knowledge distillation. 基于归一化熵评分与领域知识蒸馏的专业领域大模型生成文本零样本检测。

Python 9
ai-top-conf-2026-survey Research Tooling 科研工具

15,800 papers from CVPR / ICLR / NeurIPS auto-classified by topic with interactive visualization and automated survey tables. CVPR / ICLR / NeurIPS 共 15,800 篇论文的自动主题分类、交互可视化与自动化综述表格。

Python
ShieldDocs Legal AI 法律 AI

Localized LLM-driven document anonymization for privacy-sensitive legal workflows. 本地化 LLM 驱动的文档匿名化，面向隐私敏感的法律工作流。

Python 1
Research Value 研究价值
Post-Training & Alignment 后训练与对齐

Reward design and RL loops for multimodal LLMs 多模态大模型的奖励设计与 RL 闭环

V-FIPO extends token-level credit assignment in RLVR from instant visual dependence to future-discounted visual grounding; Forgery-aware GSPO grounds reasoning on visual evidence. Med-Banana's editor–verifier–refiner pipeline turns accepted/rejected trajectories into DPO-ready training signal. V-FIPO 将 RLVR 的 token 级信用分配从「即时视觉依赖」扩展到「未来折扣视觉锚定」；Forgery-aware GSPO 以视觉证据约束模型推理；Med-Banana 的编辑-验证-精炼流程将成功/失败轨迹转化为可直接用于 DPO 的训练信号。

ACL 2026 Main · GSPO · V-FIPO (ICLR 2026 sub) ACL 2026 主会议 · GSPO · V-FIPO（ICLR 2026 在投）
Verifier & Eval Design 验证器与评测设计

Evidence-grounded evaluation under domain shift 领域偏移下的证据驱动评测

DivScore demonstrates zero-shot verifier design: a normalized-entropy scorer with theoretical guarantees that generalizes across medical and legal domains without domain-specific labels. LLM-as-Judge rubrics drive Med-Banana's quality control. DivScore 展示零样本验证器设计：一种带理论保证的归一化熵评分器，无需领域特定标注即可泛化至医疗与法律领域。LLM-as-Judge 评分准则驱动 Med-Banana 的质量控制。

EMNLP 2025 Main · +14.4% AUROC · +64.0% recall EMNLP 2025 主会议 · +14.4% AUROC · +64.0% 召回
Agents & Systems Agent 与系统

Agent harnesses with verification loops 带验证闭环的 Agent 框架

Office Agent closes the loop on document editing: plan → act → real Word/Excel render → independent visual verifier → targeted repair, with budgeted retries, snapshots/undo, and prompt-injection defense. Plus a LangChain trading agent and quantized serving work. Office Agent 打通文档编辑的完整闭环：计划 → 执行 → 真实 Word/Excel 渲染 → 独立视觉验证器 → 定向修复，并内置预算化重试、快照/撤销与提示注入防御；另有 LangChain 交易 Agent 与量化推理部署工作。

1K+ downloads / week 1 · render-verify-repair loop 首周 1K+ 下载 · 渲染-验证-修复闭环
Selected Papers & Open Research Assets 精选论文与开放研究资产 (view all ) （查看全部 ）
Looking Ahead to Stay Grounded: Future-Discounted Visual Credit Assignment for VLM Reinforcement Learning

Zhihui Chen, Yike Yun, et al.

ICLR 2026 submission Under Review First Author

V-FIPO targets a core failure mode of RLVR post-training for vision-language models: token-level credit assignment that only rewards instant visual dependence, which lets models drift into ungrounded reasoning over long horizons. We extend credit assignment to future-discounted visual grounding — a token earns visual credit not only for the evidence it uses now, but for how that evidence supports discounted future reasoning steps — encouraging policies that stay anchored to the image across multi-step chains of thought. Developed during my Multimodal Agent RL research internship at ByteDance (Singapore).

Looking Ahead to Stay Grounded: Future-Discounted Visual Credit Assignment for VLM Reinforcement Learning

Zhihui Chen, Yike Yun, et al.

ICLR 2026 submission Under Review First Author

V-FIPO targets a core failure mode of RLVR post-training for vision-language models: token-level credit assignment that only rewards instant visual dependence, which lets models drift into ungrounded reasoning over long horizons. We extend credit assignment to future-discounted visual grounding — a token earns visual credit not only for the evidence it uses now, but for how that evidence supports discounted future reasoning steps — encouraging policies that stay anchored to the image across multi-step chains of thought. Developed during my Multimodal Agent RL research internship at ByteDance (Singapore).

MedForge: Interpretable Medical Deepfake Detection via Forgery-aware Reasoning 🔗

Zhihui Chen, Kai He, Qingyuan Lei, Bin Pu, Jian Zhang, Yuling Xu, Mengling Feng# (# corresponding author 通讯作者)

Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics (ACL) 2026 Main Conference

As generative models improve, medical deepfakes that implant or remove lesions while staying visually plausible pose growing risks to clinical safety and the integrity of medical evidence. Most prior work reduces detection to binary real-vs-fake scoring with little insight into where manipulation occurs or why. We present MedForge, an interpretable framework that introduces MedForge-90K—the first large-scale explainable medical deepfake dataset spanning CT, MRI, and X-ray, covering 19 lesion types with forgeries from 10 state-of-the-art deepfake models, each paired with expert-guided localization and clinical-grade explanations—and MedForge-Reasoner, a detector trained with a Localize-then-Analyze chain-of-thought paradigm and Forgery-aware GSPO reinforcement learning. MedForge-Reasoner achieves state-of-the-art detection while producing localized, verifiable medical rationales.

[Paper] [Project Page] [Model] [Dataset]

MedForge: Interpretable Medical Deepfake Detection via Forgery-aware Reasoning 🔗

Zhihui Chen, Kai He, Qingyuan Lei, Bin Pu, Jian Zhang, Yuling Xu, Mengling Feng# (# corresponding author 通讯作者)

Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics (ACL) 2026 Main Conference

As generative models improve, medical deepfakes that implant or remove lesions while staying visually plausible pose growing risks to clinical safety and the integrity of medical evidence. Most prior work reduces detection to binary real-vs-fake scoring with little insight into where manipulation occurs or why. We present MedForge, an interpretable framework that introduces MedForge-90K—the first large-scale explainable medical deepfake dataset spanning CT, MRI, and X-ray, covering 19 lesion types with forgeries from 10 state-of-the-art deepfake models, each paired with expert-guided localization and clinical-grade explanations—and MedForge-Reasoner, a detector trained with a Localize-then-Analyze chain-of-thought paradigm and Forgery-aware GSPO reinforcement learning. MedForge-Reasoner achieves state-of-the-art detection while producing localized, verifiable medical rationales.

[Paper] [Project Page] [Model] [Dataset]

DivScore: Zero-Shot Detection of LLM-Generated Text in Specialized Domains 🔗

Zhihui Chen, Kai He, Yucheng Huang, Yunxiao Zhu, Mengling Feng

Conference on Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing (EMNLP) 2025 Main Conference

Detecting LLM-generated text in specialized and high-stakes domains like medicine and law is crucial for combating misinformation and ensuring authenticity. We propose DivScore, a zero-shot detection framework using normalized entropy-based scoring and domain knowledge distillation to robustly identify LLM-generated text in specialized domains. Experiments show that DivScore consistently outperforms state-of-the-art detectors, with 14.4% higher AUROC and 64.0% higher recall at 0.1% false positive rate threshold.

[Paper] [Code] [Project Page]

DivScore: Zero-Shot Detection of LLM-Generated Text in Specialized Domains 🔗

Zhihui Chen, Kai He, Yucheng Huang, Yunxiao Zhu, Mengling Feng

Conference on Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing (EMNLP) 2025 Main Conference

Detecting LLM-generated text in specialized and high-stakes domains like medicine and law is crucial for combating misinformation and ensuring authenticity. We propose DivScore, a zero-shot detection framework using normalized entropy-based scoring and domain knowledge distillation to robustly identify LLM-generated text in specialized domains. Experiments show that DivScore consistently outperforms state-of-the-art detectors, with 14.4% higher AUROC and 64.0% higher recall at 0.1% false positive rate threshold.

[Paper] [Code] [Project Page]

Med-Banana-50K: A Large-Scale Cross-Modality Dataset for Medical Image Editing 🔗

Zhihui Chen, et al.

arXiv preprint 2025 EMNLP 2026 Submission · OpenReview Top 10%

Recent advances in multimodal large language models have enabled remarkable medical image editing capabilities. However, the research community's progress remains constrained by the absence of large-scale, high-quality, and openly accessible datasets built specifically for medical image editing with strict anatomical and clinical constraints. We introduce Med-Banana-50K, a comprehensive dataset for instruction-based medical image editing spanning chest X-ray, brain MRI, and fundus photography across 23 disease types. The public release includes 50,635 successful bidirectional edits and 37,822 failed attempts with full conversation logs, enabling evaluation, preference learning, and alignment research for medically grounded image editing systems.

[Paper] [Code] [Dataset] [Project Page]

Med-Banana-50K: A Large-Scale Cross-Modality Dataset for Medical Image Editing 🔗

Zhihui Chen, et al.

arXiv preprint 2025 EMNLP 2026 Submission · OpenReview Top 10%

Recent advances in multimodal large language models have enabled remarkable medical image editing capabilities. However, the research community's progress remains constrained by the absence of large-scale, high-quality, and openly accessible datasets built specifically for medical image editing with strict anatomical and clinical constraints. We introduce Med-Banana-50K, a comprehensive dataset for instruction-based medical image editing spanning chest X-ray, brain MRI, and fundus photography across 23 disease types. The public release includes 50,635 successful bidirectional edits and 37,822 failed attempts with full conversation logs, enabling evaluation, preference learning, and alignment research for medically grounded image editing systems.

[Paper] [Code] [Dataset] [Project Page]
